title: Existence and stability of steady states of a reaction convection   diffusion equation modeling microtubule formation
summary:
We generalize the Dogterom-Leibler model for microtubule dynamics [DL] to the
case where the rates of elongation as well as the lifetimes of the elongating
and shortening phases are a function of GTP-tubulin concentration. We study
also the effect of nucleation rate in the form of a damping term which leads to
new steady-states. For this model, we study existence and stability of steady
states satisfying the boundary conditions at x = 0. Our stability analysis
introduces numerical and analytical Evans function computations as a new
mathematical tool in the study of microtubule dynamics.

title: Convergence of Dirichlet Eigenvalues for Elliptic Systems on Perturbed   Domains
summary:
We consider the eigenvalues of an elliptic operator for systems with bounded,
measurable, and symmetric coefficients. We assume we have two non-empty, open,
disjoint, and bounded sets and add a set of small measure to form the perturbed
domain. Then we show that the Dirichlet eigenvalues corresponding to the family
of perturbed domains converge to the Dirichlet eigenvalues corresponding to the
unperturbed domain. Moreover, our rate of convergence is independent of the
eigenvalues. In this paper, we consider the Lam\'{e} system, systems which
satisfy a strong ellipticity condition, and systems which satisfy a
Legendre-Hadamard ellipticity condition.

title: On the BBM-Burgers Equation: Well-posedness, Ill-posedness and Long   Period Limit
comment:
This paper has been withdrawn by the author due to an small error in
  equation 5.4
summary:
In this work we study a dispersive equation with a dissipative term, the
Benjamin-Bona-Mahony-Burgers equation. First we prove that the initial value
problem for this equation is well-posed in $H^s(\mathbb{R}),$ for $s\geq 0$ and
ill-posed if $s< 0.$ The ill-posedness is in the sense that the flow-map cannot
be continuous at the origin from $H^s(\mathbb{R})$ to even
$\mathcal{D}'(\mathbb{R}).$ Additionally, we establish an exact theory of
convergence of the periodic solutions to the continuous one, in Sobolev spaces,
as the period goes to infinity.

title: The vanishing viscosity limit for Hamilton-Jacobi equations on Networks
summary:
For a Hamilton-Jacobi equation defined on a network, we introduce its
vanishing viscosity approximation. The elliptic equation is given on the edges
and coupled with Kirchhoff-type conditions at the transition vertices. We prove
that there exists exactly one solution of this elliptic approximation and
mainly that, as the viscosity vanishes, it converges to the unique solution of
the original problem.
